records.ety: [OE. pourtraien, OF. portraire, pourtraire, F. portraire, fr. L. protrahere, protractum, to draw or drag forth; pro forward, forth + trahere to draw. See Trace, v. t., and cf. Protract.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. portrayed (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Portraying.]

defs: To paint or draw the likeness of; as, to portray a king on horseback.
quotes: Take a tile, and lay it before thee, and portray upon it the city, even Jerusalem.

defs: Hence, figuratively, to describe in words.

quotes: Spear and helmets thronged, and shields Various with boastful arguments potrayed.
